title: Relations, Rows, Domains, Keys, and Missing Information · Relational Lens: Null Meaning
summary: Support tickets, teams, members, and events must become a precise relational model without duplicate identity or invented missing values. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql null meaning increment in a key-and-null-aware relational model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent unknown, missing, inapplicable, and not-yet-known values without substituting misleading sentinels.
Model optional and required references with foreign keys and predict update or delete consequences.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Model teams, members, tickets, and events where members may be unassigned, ticket titles repeat, and one ticket has 5 events. Declare row grain, candidate keys, cardinalities, NULL meaning, dependencies, and lossless relationships. A defensible solution applies Null Meaning, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a key-and-null-aware relational model.

content.code:
SELECT t.ticket_id, t.owner_id, m.name AS owner_name
FROM tickets AS t
LEFT JOIN members AS m ON m.member_id = t.owner_id
WHERE t.owner_id IS NULL OR m.active = 1
ORDER BY t.ticket_id;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

ORDER BY ticket_id;
